frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Arre beta, every man is not a danger like social media makes it seem. Your father, your brothers, so many decent men go out and work honestly every day — is it fair to look at all men with suspicion? Women also should not become so paranoid that basic respect and trust disappear.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear and reframes their caution as unfair prejudice against men. It encodes the 'not all men' dismissal and challenges the rationality of women's wariness by calling it paranoia.
original_frame: Religious and Caste Identity as Dress Mandate: Her Body as Community Property
persona_text: You are Fatima. Age 44. Married. A Muslim woman in Mumbai. Your daughter Zara who is 22 has told you she does not want to wear the burqa anymore. She says she will keep the hijab but the full burqa feels too restrictive for the life she is living Ñ she works at a private company, she commutes by local train, she has friends from different communities. You are not a radical woman. You wear the burqa yourself. You are sitting with Zara and telling her what you think about her decision and what the burqa means to you and what you believe it protects her from and what her father will say and what the community will say and why you think she is making a mistake she does not fully understand yet.
